This stage is the TUI verification layer: it sits outside normal startup and main-loop execution and proves that the terminal interface behaves correctly under realistic events, rendering constraints, and integration boundaries. At its base, shared harness code in tui/src/test_support.rs, tui/src/app/test_support.rs, tui/src/chatwidget/tests.rs, tui/src/chatwidget/tests/helpers.rs, and the VT100 backends in tui/src/test_backend.rs and tui/tests/test_backend.rs build realistic App and ChatWidget fixtures, normalize platform-specific output, and render screens without touching a real terminal.

The app-focused tests then validate orchestration across startup, session routing, replay, shutdown, config updates, model catalog and summary decisions, history rendering, auth/config migration messaging, update-cache behavior, and custom prompt submission timing. The large ChatWidget suite drives the interactive core of the TUI: composer submission, slash commands, goals, approvals, Guardian reviews, permissions, plan/review/side-thread modes, MCP startup, usage/status commands, popups, terminal title, status surfaces, layout, token charts, and replay reconstruction. Supporting renderer tests cover markdown, history cells, flex layout, and status output. Finally, tui/tests/all.rs and suite modules assemble integration smoke tests, including VT100 history behavior, ANSI sanitization, resize/reflow tmux checks, live-row commit regressions, and architectural boundary enforcement.
